goal: Fix iOS SPM build failure: @capacitor-community/apple-sign-in pins capacitor-swift-pm <8, unresolvable on Capacitor 8
root_cause: @capacitor-community/apple-sign-in tops out at v7.1.0; its Package.swift pins capacitor-swift-pm to 7.x while Capacitor 8 plugins (local-notifications) need 8.x → 'Could not resolve package dependencies' at xcodebuild package-graph stage. Blocks the whole iOS build.
fix: Removed the plugin (npm uninstall). Apple sign-in now uses the SAME system-browser OAuth flow as Google: generalized loginGoogleNative → loginViaSystemBrowser(client, provider) (signInWithOAuth skipBrowserRedirect + @capacitor/browser + appUrlOpen deep-link com.meowtarot.app://auth-callback → exchangeCodeForSession). Deleted the orphaned js/vendor/apple-sign-in.js. cap sync → 3 plugins, all Cap-8. Also added PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy to the App target's resources build phase (via xcodeproj gem).
verified: Isolated xcodebuild (iphonesimulator, CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO): capacitor-swift-pm 8.3.4 resolves, ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **, 0 errors. node --check js/auth.js passes. md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Trade-off: Apple login opens the Safari sheet, not the native iOS Sign-in-with-Apple sheet — still satisfies App Store Guideline 4.8. Needs Supabase Apple provider configured (Services ID/key) to actually complete.
